description: Rebasing a branch others have already pulled rewrites commit hashes, forcing every collaborator into painful re-syncing and duplicate commits.
body: Failure mode: an interactive or onto rebase is run on a branch that teammates already based work on, so the original commits are replaced by new hashes and everyone's local branch diverges. Detection signals: collaborators see merge conflicts against commits they thought were identical; duplicated changes appear after they merge; git pull produces a tangled history. Do not rebase a branch once it is shared. Safer fallback: merge the base into the shared branch instead of rebasing, or if a rewrite is truly required, announce it and have everyone reset to the new tip deliberately.
